summary: ISS-1496 is duplicated in open-issues.json (status='assigned-analyst') and closed-issues.json (status='fixed', fixed_at=2026-04-24T17:31:04Z, fixed_by='decider-self-apply'). Substantive work is complete: all four patches from EXP-243 landed in data/wins.json on Apr 24 (workspace md5 matches GitHub raw). This run verified all 10 substring expectations pass against current wins.json. The open-issues entry is stale and should be removed so the issue does not keep re-triggering the analyst's assigned-issues check on every run.
detail: Per CLAUDE.md the decider owns open-issues.json and closed-issues.json; the analyst's role here is to signal that the open-side entry is duplicate residue, not pending work. Confirmation that all four patches landed (verified 2026-04-25T04:xxZ in this run): H1 dvt 'conservative upper bound adopted in the absence of a paper-tabulated attenuation factor for >50° CGM stations' present; H1 dvt 'first-order, citation-honest pre-registration' present; old H1 parenthetical 'consistent with Kim & Chang (2018)\'s published attenuation envelope for latitudes outside the primary response zone' absent; H2 dvt 'approximately common multiplicative error on both residuals at first order' present; old H2 'drift both residuals in parallel' absent; H3 detail_evidence '0.6% at seven of nine' present; H3 detail_evidence '2.78% at the worst' present; old H3 '0.1% at seven stations' absent; H4 dvt 'a broad family of smooth monotonic H(r) functions fitted to the same sun/moon altitude calibration targets' present; old H4 universal 'any smooth monotonic H(r) ... produces a latitude-dependent ratio of the same form' absent. Operative verdict 'Std Model Explains' unchanged. Side-effect: monitor/analyst/processed-proposals.json does not list proposal-ISS-1496-resolution.json — the original close path appears to have skipped both the open-list deletion and the processed-list append. Decider should: (1) remove ISS-1496 from open-issues.json; (2) optionally append proposal-ISS-1496-resolution.json to processed-proposals.json so the orphan-proposal scan does not re-pick it.
do_not: Do NOT re-apply the four EXP-243 patches — they are already in wins.json. A re-application would either no-op (wins.json already matches the 'after' state) or, worse, fail loud because the 'before' substrings are no longer present.
Do NOT re-route this to curmudgeon for c10 yet — c10 is the curmudgeon's own decision based on its tracker; this proposal is a metadata-cleanup signal only.
Do NOT downgrade the original ISS-1496 in closed-issues.json — that record is the canonical resolution and should remain as-is.
